LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT CHASE BANK, USA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT (OF ELISE CAMPEAU) IS OVERRULED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS BASED ON THE ERRONEOUS PREMISE THAT THE CHALLENGED CAUSES OF ACTION WERE PREEMPTED BY FEDERAL LAW. HOWEVER, C.C. 1785.25(A) IS NOT PREEMPTED, AND NO CAUSE OF ACTION RELIES SOLELY ON PROVISIONS THAT ARE PREEMPTED. NEW THEORIES MAY NOT BE RAISED IN THE REPLY. DEMURRER IS NOT AN APPROPRIATE VEHICLE TO CHALLENGE PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
DEFENDANT(S) AND CROSS COMPLAINANT(S),ELISE CAMPEAU, REJECTED PROPOSED ARBITRATOR PAUL JOHN DION.

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT CHASE BANK, USA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT (of Elise Campeau). Appearances: Elizabeth A. Sperling of Alston & Bird LLP (213.576.1000) for cross-defendant and moving party. No other appearances. The matter was not argued as there was no compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: overruled, 10 days leave to answer. Cross-defendant's motion is based on the erroneous premise that the challenged causes of action were preempted by federal law. However, C.C. 1785.25(a) is not preempted, and no cause of action relies solely on provisions that are preempted. New theories may not be raised in the reply. Demurrer is not an appropriate vehicle to challenge part of a cause of action. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
